mnemonics: Use BREAK to Break out of the loop entirely, CONTINUE to Skip the current cycle and proceed to the Next iteration, and RETURN to Exit the function and send a value back.
keyPoints: The 'break' statement terminates the nearest enclosing loop.
The 'continue' statement skips the rest of the code inside the loop for the current iteration and moves to the next.
The 'return' statement exits a function and optionally passes back an expression to the caller.
commonMistakes: Using 'break' or 'continue' outside of a loop, which causes a SyntaxError.
Confusing 'break' (exits the loop) with 'continue' (skips to next iteration).
Placing code after a 'return' statement that will never be executed (dead code).
